approved_control.human_purpose: Move the cybernetic goal runtime from heavy frozen pre-goal execution to lean pre-goal startup plus reviewed replanning control so exploratory or research tasks can start with a small approved strategy and revise derived strategy when observations show the current strategy cannot produce the approved result.
approved_control.primary_object: The JSON-only cybernetic control chain for requirements, design, goal, plan, review, runtime, progress, amendment, generation, guard, compiler, verifier, evidence, and runtime status.
approved_control.requested_transformation: Implement lean pre-goal plus generation-aware reviewed replanning control. Lean pre-goal is the default: it creates approved target anchors, run.control.json, and gen-000/runtime.control.json with a first horizon and amendment policy instead of always generating full design/goal/plan/review artifacts. Runtime executes the current generation; when observation proves the strategy cannot produce a blocking required outcome, runtime proposes a control amendment instead of hard-completing, silently filling a slot, or immediately blocking. Reviewed amendment creates the next generation, recomputes hashes, recompiles runtime.control.json, and execution continues under that approved generation. Full pre-goal remains available only when risk, coordination, or user instruction requires it.
non_goals: Do not let runtime edit approved requirements, semantic_base, required_outcomes, what_counts_as_done, work coverage, authority, or forbidden actions in place.
Do not turn amendment into a way for runtime to change the user-approved target without human reapproval.
Do not solve this by adding only post-hoc verifier checks while keeping frozen plan execution.
Do not keep full design/goal/plan/review pre-goal as the default startup path for exploratory or research tasks.
Do not keep Markdown as official guard, compiler, runtime, or long-term dual-path control input.
Do not let old-generation evidence automatically satisfy a new generation without explicit import and review.
approved_control.how_we_know_purpose_was_met: Purpose is met when the control system can demonstrate lean pre-goal startup from requirements.control.json to run.control.json and gen-000/runtime.control.json without mandatory full design/goal/plan/review artifacts, then demonstrate an approved gen-000 runtime that observes strategy failure, writes a structured amendment proposal without changing approved anchors, routes that proposal through review, creates and activates gen-001 with hashes and runtime.control.json, and has guard/verifier/compiler/orchestrator tests proving stale generations, unresolved amendments, unauthorized anchor changes, readiness substitution, checkpoint-only substitution, and accidental full-pre-goal defaulting cannot be accepted as completion.
approved_control.what_counts_as_done: Done means JSON-only lean pre-goal and reviewed replanning are implemented end to end: lean pre-goal is the default startup path and can produce requirements.control.json, run.control.json, and gen-000/runtime.control.json without mandatory full design/goal/plan/review artifacts; full pre-goal is selected only for high-risk, irreversible, externally impactful, or explicitly full workflows; run.control.json tracks the active generation; control generations live under gen-N directories; progress events can propose, approve, reject, or block amendments; runtime can request reviewed modification when current strategy cannot produce approved outcomes; orchestrator can create a new reviewed generation without changing anchors; compiler, guard, and verifier are generation-aware; old evidence is imported or invalidated explicitly; final success is judged only by the current approved generation; historical failure regressions pass.

required_outcomes.statement: Lean pre-goal is the default startup path: it creates approved target anchors, run.control.json, and gen-000/runtime.control.json with first-horizon strategy and amendment policy without requiring complete design/goal/plan/review artifacts up front.

required_evidence.description: schemas, fixtures, or tests demonstrate a lean pre-goal run with requirements.control.json, run.control.json, and gen-000/runtime.control.json only
orchestrator or routing logic selects lean mode by default and does not require full pre-goal artifacts for exploratory work

not_satisfied_by: full design/goal/plan/review remains the default for all Level 3 work
lean mode exists only as documentation but compiler/guard/runtime still require a full control chain
runtime starts without target anchors or amendment policy

required_outcomes.statement: Full pre-goal remains available as an explicit exception for high-risk, irreversible, externally impactful, compliance-sensitive, complex-coordination, non-replannable, or user-requested full workflows.

required_evidence.description: routing/requirements/orchestrator rules define when full mode is required instead of lean mode
tests show high-risk or explicitly full workflows still require full pre-goal artifacts

not_satisfied_by: using lean mode for irreversible or externally risky target execution
letting runtime decide after launch whether the work should have been full mode

required_outcomes.statement: The official run model supports generation-aware control facts with run.control.json, current_generation, gen-N directories, per-generation review/hash/runtime, and cross-generation progress.

required_evidence.description: run.control schema and example or fixture validate generation metadata
guard rejects stale, missing, or unreviewed current_generation

not_satisfied_by: single runtime.control.json treated as the permanent final contract
a root run directory with no active generation index

required_outcomes.statement: Runtime can propose a reviewed control amendment when observation shows current strategy cannot produce a required outcome, while approved anchors remain read-only.

required_evidence.description: progress event schema supports control.amendment.proposed and related amendment events
using-control-json instructs runtime to request reviewed modification rather than hard-complete or silently fill a slot

not_satisfied_by: runtime directly edits approved control JSON
runtime can only complete or block when strategy mismatches are observed

required_outcomes.statement: Orchestration can turn an anchor-preserving amendment proposal into a reviewed next generation and must stop for human approval when anchors would change.

required_evidence.description: orchestrator supports amendment proposal intake, anchor checks, review, guard, runtime compile, and current_generation update
tests show semantic_base, required_outcomes, what_counts_as_done, authority, or work coverage changes require human approval

not_satisfied_by: manual hand-created gen-001 without reviewed amendment
automatic amendment that changes approved anchors

required_outcomes.statement: Compiler, guard, and verifier operate on the current approved generation and reject superseded, unresolved, stale, or inconsistent generations.

required_evidence.description: compiler accepts run root plus generation or current_generation and emits pointer-only runtime command
verifier judges only current generation and rejects unresolved amendments
guard validates generation completeness, hashes, parent amendment source, and current generation status

not_satisfied_by: guard/compiler/verifier continue assuming one control chain per run root
final report can claim success while unresolved amendment proposals exist

required_outcomes.statement: New generations explicitly import or invalidate evidence from previous generations, and old completed steps do not automatically satisfy new generation requirements.

required_evidence.description: tests show gen-000 steps/evidence do not automatically satisfy gen-001

not_satisfied_by: old generation progress automatically counts for new generation completion

required_outcomes.statement: Regressions cover historical semantic failures: api/v2 readiness cannot replace implementation, checkpoint-only path cannot be wrapped as full workflow ceiling, unresolved amendment blocks success, and stale current_generation is rejected.

required_evidence.description: regression forces amendment or failure when readiness replaces api/v2 implementation
regression forces amendment or failure when checkpoint-only path is wrapped as full workflow ceiling
regression rejects goal_achieved true with unresolved amendment

not_satisfied_by: tests do not encode the historical failure modes

final_answer_format.medium: chat summary plus JSON control artifacts and test evidence
